description: This figure presents a bibliometric analysis showing the temporal evolution of research publications related to lightweight steels from 2010 to 2022, based on the Scopus database. The line chart displays two distinct search topics: \"Light-weight steel\" (blue curve) and \"Fe-Mn-Al-C steel\" (green curve), with the y-axis representing search results in thousands and the x-axis representing the search year. Both research topics demonstrate a consistent upward trend over the 12-year period, indicating growing scientific interest in these material systems. The \"Light-weight steel\" category shows a steeper increase, rising from approximately 13,000 publications in 2010 to around 48,000 in 2022. Meanwhile, \"Fe-Mn-Al-C steel\" exhibits steady growth from roughly 8,000 to 30,000 publications during the same period. This quantitative data evidences the accelerating research momentum in lightweight Fe-Mn-Al-C steels, supporting the review paper's focus on these alloys as emerging materials for automotive and structural applications aimed at improving fuel efficiency and reducing CO2 emissions through density reduction.
